# ApexCSS Framework - LLM Documentation
# Version: 0.4.0
# Last Updated: 2026-04-11
# Purpose: Comprehensive reference for AI/LLM consumption
# Format: Plain text with structured sections for LLM parsing

================================================================================
SECTION 1: FRAMEWORK OVERVIEW
================================================================================

ApexCSS is a utility-first CSS framework built with Sass/SCSS, inspired by Tailwind
CSS but with a modular, configuration-driven architecture. It provides 500+
utility classes across 104+ feature categories.

KEY CHARACTERISTICS:
- Built entirely with Sass (not PostCSS-based like Tailwind)
- Uses OKLCH color space for perceptually uniform colors
- Modular architecture: import only what you need
- JavaScript-based configuration (apex.config.js)
- CSS custom properties for runtime theming
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Full dark mode and RTL support
- Accessibility-focused (screen readers, focus management, reduced motion)

PROJECT STATUS: Pre-release, actively developed

================================================================================
SECTION 2: QUICK START
================================================================================

INSTALLATION:
    npm install apexcss

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VITE PROJECT SETUP
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. INSTALL VITE (if not already installed):
    npm install --save-dev vite

2. CREATE VITE CONFIGURATION (vite.config.js):
    import { defineConfig } from 'vite'

    export default defineConfig({
      // Vite automatically resolves bare CSS imports from node_modules
    })

3. ADD SCRIPTS TO package.json:
    {
      "scripts": {
        "dev": "vite",
        "build": "vite build",
        "preview": "vite preview"
      }
    }

4. CREATE ENTRY HTML FILE (index.html):
    <!DOCTYPE html>
    <html lang="en">
    <head>
        <meta charset="UTF-8" />
        <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
        <title>My Apex Project</title>
        <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/main.css" />
    </head>
    <body>
        <!-- Your content here -->
    </body>
    </html>

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMPORT OPTIONS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OPTION 1: FULL IMPORT
    Import the complete Apex CSS framework:

    /* css/main.css */
    @import 'apexcss';

OPTION 2: INDIVIDUAL LAYERS (RECOMMENDED)
    Import specific layers with cascade layer assignment:

    /* css/main.css */
    @layer base, utilities, themes;

    @import 'apexcss/base' layer(base);
    @import 'apexcss/utilities' layer(utilities);
    @import 'apexcss/themes' layer(themes);

    This approach allows you to:
    - Control the order of cascade layers
    - Add your own styles to each layer
    - Optimize bundle size by importing only what you need

OPTION 3: SPECIFIC FILES
    Import individual files directly:

    @import 'apexcss/base';      /* Base styles (resets, typography) */
    @import 'apexcss/utilities'; /* Utility classes */
    @import 'apexcss/themes';    /* Theme variables and styles */

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PACKAGE EXPORTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Export Path           | Description                           |
|-----------------------|---------------------------------------|
| apexcss               | Full framework (all layers combined)  |
| apexcss/base          | Base styles, resets, typography       |
| apexcss/utilities     | 500+ utility classes                  |
| apexcss/themes        | Theme variables and color schemes     |

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RUNNING THE PROJECT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Start development server:
    npm run dev

Build for production:
    npm run build

Preview production build:
    npm run preview

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASCADE LAYERS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Apex CSS uses CSS Cascade Layers (@layer) for predictable specificity:

1. base - Resets and foundational styles (lowest specificity)
2. utilities - Utility classes
3. themes - Theme variables and color schemes

When using individual layer imports, define your layer order first:
    @layer base, utilities, themes;

This ensures proper cascade order regardless of import order.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDING CUSTOM STYLES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Extend Apex CSS by adding your own styles in the appropriate layer:
    @layer base, utilities, themes;

    @import 'apexcss/base' layer(base);
    @import 'apexcss/utilities' layer(utilities);
    @import 'apexcss/themes' layer(themes);

    /* Your custom utilities */
    @layer utilities {
      .my-custom-class {
        /* styles */
      }
    }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FRAMEWORK INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

REACT (VITE):
    1. Create project:
       npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react
       cd my-app
       npm install apexcss

    2. Import in CSS (src/index.css or src/App.css):
       @layer base, utilities, themes;

       @import 'apexcss/base' layer(base);
       @import 'apexcss/utilities' layer(utilities);
       @import 'apexcss/themes' layer(themes);

    3. Or import in entry point (src/main.jsx):
       import 'apexcss'
       // or individual layers:
       // import 'apexcss/base'
       // import 'apexcss/utilities'
       // import 'apexcss/themes'

VUE (VITE):
    1. Create project:
       npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template vue
       cd my-app
       npm install apexcss

    2. Import in CSS (src/style.css):
       @layer base, utilities, themes;

       @import 'apexcss/base' layer(base);
       @import 'apexcss/utilities' layer(utilities);
       @import 'apexcss/themes' layer(themes);

    3. Or import in entry point (src/main.js):
       import 'apexcss'

ANGULAR:
    1. Create project:
       ng new my-app
       cd my-app
       npm install apexcss

    2. Add to angular.json styles array:
       {
         "projects": {
           "my-app": {
             "architect": {
               "build": {
                 "options": {
                   "styles": [
                     "node_modules/apexcss/dist/apex.css",
                     "src/styles.css"
                   ]
                 }
               }
             }
           }
         }
       }

    3. Or import in global styles (src/styles.css):
       @layer base, utilities, themes;

       @import 'apexcss/base' layer(base);
       @import 'apexcss/utilities' layer(utilities);
       @import 'apexcss/themes' layer(themes);

SVELTE (VITE):
    1. Create project:
       npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template svelte
       cd my-app
       npm install apexcss

    2. Import in CSS (src/app.css):
       @layer base, utilities, themes;

       @import 'apexcss/base' layer(base);
       @import 'apexcss/utilities' layer(utilities);
       @import 'apexcss/themes' layer(themes);

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LEGACY USAGE (DIRECT LINK)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Option 1: Full framework
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="node_modules/apexcss/dist/apex.css">

Option 2: Individual modules (tree-shaking)
    @use 'apexcss/dist/base.css';       /* Reset/base styles only */
    @use 'apexcss/dist/utilities.css'; /* Utility classes only */
    @use 'apexcss/dist/themes.css';    /* Theme variables only */

MINIMAL HTML EXAMPLE:
    <!DOCTYPE html>
    <html lang="en">
    <head>
        <link rel="stylesheet" href="dist/apex.css">
    </head>
    <body class="bg-gray-50 text-gray-900 font-sans p-4">
        <div class="max-w-md mx-auto bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md p-6">
            <h1 class="text-2xl font-bold text-primary-600 mb-4">Hello ApexCSS</h1>
            <p class="text-gray-600 mb-4">Utility-first CSS framework</p>
            <button class="bg-primary-500 hover:bg-primary-600 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded-md transition-colors">
                Get Started
            </button>
        </div>
    </body>
    </html>

================================================================================
SECTION 3: CONFIGURATION SYSTEM
================================================================================

CONFIGURATION FILE: apex.config.js (project root)

The framework uses a JavaScript configuration file that generates SCSS variables.
Workflow: Edit apex.config.js → npm run config:build → npm run build

104 FEATURE TOGGLES (enable/disable utility categories):

Core Layout:
    display: true          /* Display utilities (block, flex, grid, hidden) */
    flexbox: true          /* Flexbox layout utilities */
    grid: true             /* CSS Grid layout utilities */
    positioning: true      /* Position utilities (static, relative, absolute) */
    visibility: true       /* Visibility utilities */

Core Spacing:
    spacing: true          /* Margin and padding utilities */
    sizing: true           /* Width and height utilities */

Core Typography:
    typography: true       /* Basic typography utilities */

Core Visual:
    colors: true           /* Text color utilities */
    backgrounds: true      /* Background color utilities */
    borders: true          /* Border width, style, color utilities */
    shadows: true          /* Box shadow utilities */
    opacity: true          /* Opacity utilities */
    overflow: true         /* Overflow utilities */
    objectFit: true        /* Object-fit utilities */

Core Interaction:
    cursor: true           /* Cursor utilities */
    transitions: true      /* Transition utilities */

Extended Layout:
    flexExtended: true     /* Extended flex (grow, shrink, basis) */
    gridExtended: true     /* Extended grid (auto-flow, template areas) */
    float: true            /* Float utilities */
    containerQueries: true /* CSS Container Query utilities */
    isolation: true        /* Isolation utilities */
    placeItems: true       /* Place-items and place-content */
    justifyItems: true     /* Justify-items utilities */
    spaceBetween: true     /* Space-between utilities */
    columns: true          /* CSS columns utilities */
    columnsExtended: true  /* Extended column utilities */

Extended Typography:
    typographyExtended: true
    fontExtended: true
    letterSpacing: true
    lineHeight: true
    textAlignLast: true
    textDecorationExtended: true
    textJustify: true
    textIndent: true
    textShadow: true
    textEmphasis: true
    textOrientation: true
    textUnderline: true
    hangingPunctuation: true
    hyphenate: true
    initialLetter: true
    tabSize: true
    wordBreak: true
    wordWrap: true
    writingMode: true
    unicodeBidi: true

Extended Visual:
    backgroundExtended: true
    colorModifiers: false   /* Disabled by default */
    blendModes: true
    masks: true
    borderRadiusLogical: true
    ring: true              /* Focus ring utilities */
    outline: true
    appearance: true
    accentColor: true
    colorScheme: true

Extended Interaction:
    interaction: true
    userSelect: true
    willChange: true
    all: true
    caret: true
    scroll: true
    overscrollBehavior: true
    overscrollBehaviorExtended: true
    overflowExtended: true

Effects & Animations:
    animations: true
    transforms: true
    transforms3d: true
    filters: true
    aspectRatio: true
    imageRendering: true
    transitionBehavior: true

Content Utilities:
    list: true
    listStyleExtended: true
    table: true
    counter: true
    caption: true
    quotes: true
    orphans: true
    widows: true
    pageBreak: true
    break: true
    verticalAlign: true

Advanced Utilities:
    arbitrary: true        /* Arbitrary value support ([w-100px]) */
    logicalProperties: true /* Logical margin/padding (ms-, me-, ps-, pe-) */
    sizingLogical: true
    offset: true
    shapeOutside: true
    markerExtended: true
    zoom: true
    fieldSizing: true
    svg: true
    box: true
    divide: true

State Variants:
    states: true           /* Enable all state variants */
    hover: true
    focus: true
    active: true
    disabled: true

Theme Support:
    darkMode: true
    rtl: true
    accessibility: true
    zIndex: true

================================================================================
SECTION 4: BREAKPOINT SYSTEM
================================================================================

DEFAULT BREAKPOINTS (mobile-first):
    xs:   0px     /* All screens (mobile-first base) */
    sm:   320px   /* Small phones & Smart Watches */
    md:   768px   /* Tablets */
    lg:   1024px  /* Small laptops */
    xl:   1280px  /* Desktops */
    xxl:  2560px  /* 2K / QHD displays */
    xxxl: 3840px  /* 4K / UHD displays */
    ultra: 7680px /* 8K displays */

RESPONSIVE PREFIX SYNTAX:
    {breakpoint}:{utility}

    Examples:
        md:d-flex      /* display: flex at md breakpoint and up */
        lg:p-8         /* padding: 2rem at lg breakpoint and up */
        sm:hidden      /* display: none below sm breakpoint */

CUSTOMIZATION (in apex.config.js):
    breakpoints: {
        xs: '0px',
        sm: '320px',
        md: '768px',
        lg: '1024px',
        xl: '1280px',
        xxl: '2560px',
        xxxl: '3840px',
        ultra: '7680px'
    }

NOTE: The xs breakpoint (0px) is useful for explicit mobile-first utilities
that should apply to all screen sizes, while still allowing overrides at
larger breakpoints.

================================================================================
SECTION 5: SPACING SYSTEM
================================================================================

SPACING SCALE (based on 0.25rem increments):
    0:     0
    px:    1px
    0.5:   0.125rem  (2px)
    1:     0.25rem   (4px)
    1.5:   0.375rem  (6px)
    2:     0.5rem    (8px)
    2.5:   0.625rem  (10px)
    3:     0.75rem   (12px)
    3.5:   0.875rem  (14px)
    4:     1rem      (16px)
    5:     1.25rem   (20px)
    6:     1.5rem    (24px)
    7:     1.75rem   (28px)
    8:     2rem      (32px)
    9:     2.25rem   (36px)
    10:    2.5rem    (40px)
    11:    2.75rem   (44px)
    12:    3rem      (48px)
    14:    3.5rem    (56px)
    16:    4rem      (64px)
    20:    5rem      (80px)
    24:    6rem      (96px)
    28:    7rem      (112px)
    32:    8rem      (128px)
    36:    9rem      (144px)
    40:    10rem     (160px)
    44:    11rem     (176px)
    48:    12rem     (192px)
    52:    13rem     (208px)
    56:    14rem     (224px)
    60:    15rem     (240px)
    64:    16rem     (256px)
    72:    18rem     (288px)
    80:    20rem     (320px)
    96:    24rem     (384px)

NOTE ON DECIMAL SPACING CLASSES:
    Decimal values (0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5) use hyphenated class names
    to avoid CSS parser issues with escaped dots:

    CSS Class          Generated Class    Value
    --------------------------------------------
    p-0.5              .p-0-5             0.125rem (2px)
    p-1.5              .p-1-5             0.375rem (6px)
    p-2.5              .p-2-5             0.625rem (10px)
    p-3.5              .p-3-5             0.875rem (14px)
    m-0.5              .m-0-5             0.125rem (2px)
    m-1.5              .m-1-5             0.375rem (6px)
    etc.

    This applies to all spacing utilities: margin (m-*, mx-*, my-*, etc.),
    padding (p-*, px-*, py-*, etc.), and gap utilities.

SPACING UTILITY PATTERNS:
    /* Margin */
    m-{value}       /* All sides */
    mt-{value}      /* Top */
    mr-{value}      /* Right */
    mb-{value}      /* Bottom */
    ml-{value}      /* Left */
    mx-{value}      /* Horizontal (left + right) */
    my-{value}      /* Vertical (top + bottom) */

    /* Padding */
    p-{value}       /* All sides */
    pt-{value}      /* Top */
    pr-{value}      /* Right */
    pb-{value}      /* Bottom */
    pl-{value}      /* Left */
    px-{value}      /* Horizontal */
    py-{value}      /* Vertical */

    /* Gap */
    gap-{value}     /* All gaps */
    gap-x-{value}   /* Column gap */
    gap-y-{value}   /* Row gap */

AUTO SPACING:
    mx-auto         /* margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto */
    my-auto         /* margin-top: auto; margin-bottom: auto */
    m-auto          /* margin: auto */

LOGICAL PROPERTIES (RTL-aware):
    ms-{value}      /* margin-inline-start (replaces ml) */
    me-{value}      /* margin-inline-end (replaces mr) */
    ps-{value}      /* padding-inline-start (replaces pl) */
    pe-{value}      /* padding-inline-end (replaces pr) */

================================================================================
SECTION 6: COLOR SYSTEM (OKLCH)
================================================================================

ApexCSS uses the OKLCH color space for perceptually uniform colors.
Format: oklch(lightness% chroma hue)

DEFAULT COLOR PALETTE:

Primary (Blue):
    text-primary-50  through text-primary-950
    bg-primary-50    through bg-primary-950
    border-primary-50 through border-primary-950

Gray (Neutral):
    text-gray-50     through text-gray-950
    bg-gray-50       through bg-gray-950

Success (Green):
    text-success-50  through text-success-950
    bg-success-50    through bg-success-950

Warning (Amber):
    text-warning-50  through text-warning-950
    bg-warning-50    through bg-warning-950

Danger (Red):
    text-danger-50   through text-danger-950
    bg-danger-50     through bg-danger-950

COLOR CONFIGURATION (in apex.config.js):
    colors: {
        primary: {
            hue: 250,           /* 0-360 on color wheel */
            chroma: 0.18,       /* 0-0.4 intensity */
            lightnessScale: {
                50: 95,         /* Lightest */
                100: 90,
                200: 85,
                300: 78,
                400: 70,
                500: 62,        /* Base color */
                600: 55,
                700: 45,
                800: 35,
                900: 25,
                950: 20         /* Darkest */
            }
        }
    }

OPACITY MODIFIERS:
    bg-primary-500/50    /* 50% opacity background */
    text-primary-500/75  /* 75% opacity text */

================================================================================
SECTION 7: CORE UTILITY REFERENCE
================================================================================

----- DISPLAY -----
    block            /* display: block */
    inline-block     /* display: inline-block */
    inline           /* display: inline */
    flex             /* display: flex */
    inline-flex      /* display: inline-flex */
    grid             /* display: grid */
    inline-grid      /* display: inline-grid */
    table            /* display: table */
    hidden           /* display: none */
    contents         /* display: contents */
    list-item        /* display: list-item */

----- FLEXBOX -----
    /* Direction */
    flex-row         /* flex-direction: row */
    flex-row-reverse /* flex-direction: row-reverse */
    flex-col         /* flex-direction: column */
    flex-col-reverse /* flex-direction: column-reverse */

    /* Wrap */
    flex-wrap        /* flex-wrap: wrap */
    flex-nowrap      /* flex-wrap: nowrap */
    flex-wrap-reverse/* flex-wrap: wrap-reverse */

    /* Grow/Shrink */
    flex-grow        /* flex-grow: 1 */
    flex-grow-0      /* flex-grow: 0 */
    flex-shrink      /* flex-shrink: 1 */
    flex-shrink-0    /* flex-shrink: 0 */

    /* Basis */
    flex-basis-0     /* flex-basis: 0 */
    flex-basis-auto  /* flex-basis: auto */
    flex-basis-full  /* flex-basis: 100% */
    flex-basis-1/2   /* flex-basis: 50% */
    flex-basis-1/3   /* flex-basis: 33.3333% */
    flex-basis-2/3   /* flex-basis: 66.6667% */
    flex-basis-1/4   /* flex-basis: 25% */
    flex-basis-3/4   /* flex-basis: 75% */

    /* Justify Content */
    justify-start    /* justify-content: flex-start */
    justify-end      /* justify-content: flex-end */
    justify-center   /* justify-content: center */
    justify-between  /* justify-content: space-between */
    justify-around   /* justify-content: space-around */
    justify-evenly   /* justify-content: space-evenly */

    /* Align Items */
    items-start      /* align-items: flex-start */
    items-end        /* align-items: flex-end */
    items-center     /* align-items: center */
    items-baseline   /* align-items: baseline */
    items-stretch    /* align-items: stretch */

    /* Align Self */
    self-auto        /* align-self: auto */
    self-start       /* align-self: flex-start */
    self-end         /* align-self: flex-end */
    self-center      /* align-self: center */
    self-baseline    /* align-self: baseline */
    self-stretch     /* align-self: stretch */

    /* Order */
    order-1 through order-12
    order-first      /* order: -9999 */
    order-last       /* order: 9999 */
    order-none       /* order: 0 */

    /* Flex Shorthand */
    flex-1           /* flex: 1 1 0% */
    flex-auto        /* flex: 1 1 auto */
    flex-initial     /* flex: 0 1 auto */
    flex-none        /* flex: none */

----- GRID -----
    grid-cols-1 through grid-cols-12
    grid-rows-1 through grid-rows-6
    col-span-1 through col-span-12
    col-span-full
    row-span-1 through row-span-6
    col-start-1 through col-start-13
    col-end-1 through col-end-13
    row-start-1 through row-start-7
    row-end-1 through row-end-7
    gap-{value}

----- POSITIONING -----
    static           /* position: static */
    relative         /* position: relative */
    absolute         /* position: absolute */
    fixed            /* position: fixed */
    sticky           /* position: sticky */

    /* Position values (use with absolute/fixed) */
    inset-0          /* inset: 0 */
    inset-x-0        /* left: 0; right: 0 */
    inset-y-0        /* top: 0; bottom: 0 */
    top-0            /* top: 0 */
    right-0          /* right: 0 */
    bottom-0         /* bottom: 0 */
    left-0           /* left: 0 */

    /* Z-Index */
    z-0, z-10, z-20, z-30, z-40, z-50
    z-auto

----- SIZING -----
    /* Width */
    w-0 through w-96
    w-auto
    w-full           /* width: 100% */
    w-screen         /* width: 100vw */
    w-min            /* width: min-content */
    w-max            /* width: max-content */
    w-fit            /* width: fit-content */

    /* Height */
    h-0 through h-96
    h-auto
    h-full           /* height: 100% */
    h-screen         /* height: 100vh */
    h-min            /* height: min-content */
    h-max            /* height: max-content */
    h-fit            /* height: fit-content */

    /* Min/Max */
    min-w-0, min-w-full
    max-w-0 through max-w-96
    max-w-none
    min-h-0, min-h-full
    max-h-0 through max-h-96
    max-h-none
    max-w-screen-sm through max-w-screen-ultra

----- TYPOGRAPHY -----
    /* Font Size */
    text-xs          /* font-size: var(--font-size-xs) */
    text-sm          /* font-size: var(--font-size-sm) */
    text-base        /* font-size: var(--font-size-base) */
    text-lg          /* font-size: var(--font-size-lg) */
    text-xl          /* font-size: var(--font-size-xl) */
    text-2xl through text-9xl

    /* Font Weight */
    font-thin        /* font-weight: 100 */
    font-extralight  /* font-weight: 200 */
    font-light       /* font-weight: 300 */
    font-normal      /* font-weight: 400 */
    font-medium      /* font-weight: 500 */
    font-semibold    /* font-weight: 600 */
    font-bold        /* font-weight: 700 */
    font-extrabold   /* font-weight: 800 */
    font-black       /* font-weight: 900 */

    /* Line Height */
    leading-none     /* line-height: 1 */
    leading-tight    /* line-height: 1.25 */
    leading-snug     /* line-height: 1.375 */
    leading-normal   /* line-height: 1.5 */
    leading-relaxed  /* line-height: 1.625 */
    leading-loose    /* line-height: 2 */

    /* Text Align */
    text-left        /* text-align: left */
    text-center      /* text-align: center */
    text-right       /* text-align: right */
    text-justify     /* text-align: justify */
    text-start       /* text-align: start (logical) */
    text-end         /* text-align: end (logical) */

    /* Text Transform */
    uppercase
    lowercase
    capitalize
    normal-case

    /* Text Decoration */
    underline
    line-through
    no-underline
    overline

    /* Letter Spacing */
    tracking-tighter /* letter-spacing: -0.05em */
    tracking-tight   /* letter-spacing: -0.025em */
    tracking-normal  /* letter-spacing: 0 */
    tracking-wide    /* letter-spacing: 0.025em */
    tracking-wider   /* letter-spacing: 0.05em */
    tracking-widest  /* letter-spacing: 0.1em */

----- BORDERS -----
    /* Border Width */
    border           /* border-width: 1px */
    border-0         /* border-width: 0 */
    border-2         /* border-width: 2px */
    border-4         /* border-width: 4px */
    border-8         /* border-width: 8px */
    border-t-{n}, border-r-{n}, border-b-{n}, border-l-{n}
    border-x-{n}, border-y-{n}

    /* Border Style */
    border-solid
    border-dashed
    border-dotted
    border-double
    border-hidden
    border-none

    /* Border Radius */
    rounded-none     /* border-radius: 0 */
    rounded-sm       /* border-radius: var(--radius-sm) */
    rounded          /* border-radius: var(--radius-md) */
    rounded-md       /* border-radius: var(--radius-md) */
    rounded-lg       /* border-radius: var(--radius-lg) */
    rounded-xl       /* border-radius: var(--radius-xl) */
    rounded-2xl      /* border-radius: var(--radius-2xl) */
    rounded-3xl      /* border-radius: var(--radius-3xl) */
    rounded-full     /* border-radius: 9999px */
    rounded-t-{size}, rounded-r-{size}, rounded-b-{size}, rounded-l-{size}
    rounded-tl-{size}, rounded-tr-{size}, rounded-br-{size}, rounded-bl-{size}

----- SHADOWS -----
    shadow-none
    shadow-sm
    shadow           /* Default shadow */
    shadow-md
    shadow-lg
    shadow-xl
    shadow-2xl
    shadow-inner

----- OPACITY -----
    opacity-0 through opacity-100 (increments of 5)

----- OVERFLOW -----
    overflow-auto
    overflow-hidden
    overflow-visible
    overflow-scroll
    overflow-x-auto, overflow-y-auto
    overflow-x-hidden, overflow-y-hidden

----- VISIBILITY -----
    visible
    invisible        /* visibility: hidden */
    collapse         /* visibility: collapse (tables) */

----- CURSOR -----
    cursor-auto
    cursor-default
    cursor-pointer
    cursor-wait
    cursor-text
    cursor-move
    cursor-help
    cursor-not-allowed
    cursor-none

----- TRANSITIONS -----
    transition-none
    transition-all
    transition-colors
    transition-opacity
    transition-shadow
    transition-transform

    /* Duration */
    duration-0, duration-75, duration-100, duration-150
    duration-200, duration-300, duration-500, duration-700, duration-1000

    /* Timing Function */
    ease-linear
    ease-in
    ease-out
    ease-in-out

    /* Delay */
    delay-0, delay-75, delay-100, delay-150
    delay-200, delay-300, delay-500, delay-700, delay-1000

================================================================================
SECTION 8: STATE VARIANTS
================================================================================

State variants apply styles on specific element states.

SYNTAX PATTERNS:
    {state}:{utility}
    {state}:{responsive-prefix}:{utility}

AVAILABLE STATES:
    hover:           /* :hover pseudo-class */
    focus:           /* :focus pseudo-class */
    focus-visible:   /* :focus-visible pseudo-class */
    active:          /* :active pseudo-class */
    disabled:        /* :disabled pseudo-class */

USAGE EXAMPLES:
    hover:bg-primary-600      /* Change bg on hover */
    focus:outline-2           /* Show outline on focus */
    focus-visible:ring-2      /* Show ring on keyboard focus */
    active:bg-primary-700     /* Darker bg when active */
    disabled:opacity-50       /* Reduce opacity when disabled */
    hover:md:bg-primary-600   /* Hover at md breakpoint and up */

================================================================================
SECTION 9: DARK MODE
================================================================================

DARK MODE CLASSES:
    dark:{utility}

EXAMPLES:
    dark:bg-gray-900           /* Dark background in dark mode */
    dark:text-white            /* White text in dark mode */
    dark:border-gray-700       /* Darker borders in dark mode */

IMPLEMENTATION:
By default, dark mode uses the 'class' strategy. Add 'dark' class to html:
    <html class="dark">

Or use media query (system preference) by configuring in apex.config.js.

DARK MODE TOGGLE EXAMPLE:
    <button onclick="document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark')">
        Toggle Theme
    </button>

================================================================================
SECTION 10: RTL (RIGHT-TO-LEFT) SUPPORT
================================================================================

ApexCSS provides full RTL support using logical properties.

LOGICAL PROPERTIES (recommended):
    ms-{n}          /* margin-inline-start (replaces ml) */
    me-{n}          /* margin-inline-end (replaces mr) */
    ps-{n}          /* padding-inline-start (replaces pl) */
    pe-{n}          /* padding-inline-end (replaces pr) */
    text-start      /* text-align: start (replaces text-left) */
    text-end        /* text-align: end (replaces text-right) */

RTL-SPECIFIC UTILITIES:
    rtl:me-{n}      /* Only apply in RTL mode */
    ltr:ms-{n}      /* Only apply in LTR mode */

IMPLEMENTATION:
Add dir="rtl" to html element:
    <html dir="rtl">

================================================================================
SECTION 11: ACCESSIBILITY UTILITIES
================================================================================

SCREEN READER UTILITIES:
    sr-only             /* Visually hide but keep for screen readers */
    not-sr-only         /* Override sr-only */

FOCUS UTILITIES:
    focus:outline-none  /* Remove default outline */
    focus:ring-2        /* Add custom ring on focus */
    focus-visible:ring-2/* Ring only on keyboard focus */

REDUCED MOTION:
    motion-safe:{utility}   /* Only apply if user prefers motion */
    motion-reduce:{utility} /* Only apply if user prefers reduced motion */

EXAMPLE:
    <div class="motion-safe:animate-bounce motion-reduce:transform-none">
        Animated content
    </div>

================================================================================
SECTION 12: EXTENDED UTILITIES
================================================================================

----- TRANSFORMS -----
    /* Translate */
    translate-x-{n}, translate-y-{n}
    translate-x-full, translate-y-full
    -translate-x-{n} (negative values)

    /* Scale */
    scale-0 through scale-150
    scale-x-{n}, scale-y-{n}

    /* Rotate */
    rotate-0, rotate-45, rotate-90, rotate-135, rotate-180
    rotate-270, rotate-360
    -rotate-45 (negative)

    /* Skew */
    skew-x-{n}, skew-y-{n}

    /* Transform Origin */
    origin-center, origin-top, origin-right, origin-bottom, origin-left
    origin-top-left, origin-top-right, origin-bottom-left, origin-bottom-right

----- FILTERS -----
    blur-none, blur-sm, blur, blur-md, blur-lg, blur-xl, blur-2xl, blur-3xl
    brightness-0 through brightness-200
    contrast-0 through contrast-200
    saturate-0 through saturate-200
    grayscale, grayscale-0
    sepia, sepia-0
    invert, invert-0
    hue-rotate-0, hue-rotate-15, hue-rotate-30, hue-rotate-60
    hue-rotate-90, hue-rotate-180
    drop-shadow-sm, drop-shadow, drop-shadow-md, drop-shadow-lg
    drop-shadow-xl, drop-shadow-2xl

----- ASPECT RATIO -----
    aspect-auto, aspect-square, aspect-video
    aspect-[4/3], aspect-[16/9], aspect-[21/9]

----- CONTAINER QUERIES -----
    @container (min-width: ...)

    Container size utilities:
    @xs:{utility}, @sm:{utility}, @md:{utility}, @lg:{utility}, @xl:{utility}, @2xl:{utility}

----- ANIMATIONS -----
    animate-spin
    animate-ping
    animate-pulse
    animate-bounce
    animate-none

----- SCROLL UTILITIES -----
    scroll-auto, scroll-smooth
    scroll-m-{n}, scroll-mt-{n}, etc.
    scroll-p-{n}, scroll-pt-{n}, etc.

----- USER SELECT -----
    select-none, select-text, select-all, select-auto

----- POINTER EVENTS -----
    pointer-events-auto, pointer-events-none

----- RESIZE -----
    resize-none, resize, resize-x, resize-y

================================================================================
SECTION 13: SASS MIXINS API
================================================================================

For advanced users creating custom utilities.

----- UTILITY GENERATION MIXINS -----

@include generate-property-utilities($values-map, $property, $class-prefix: '');
    /* Generates utilities from a value map */
    Example:
        @include generate-property-utilities(
            (block: block, hidden: none),
            'display'
        );
    Output:
        .block { display: block; }
        .hidden { display: none; }

@include generate-declaration-utilities($utilities-map, $class-prefix: '');
    /* Generates utilities with multiple declarations */
    Example:
        @include generate-declaration-utilities((
            flex-center: (display: flex, justify-content: center, align-items: center)
        ));

@include generate-from-css-vars($class-name, $property, $var-map, $prefix: '');
    /* Generates utilities referencing CSS custom properties */
    Example:
        @include generate-from-css-vars('text', 'font-size', (
            sm: '--font-size-sm',
            base: '--font-size-base'
        ));
    Output:
        .text-sm { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); }
        .text-base { font-size: var(--font-size-base); }

@include generate-color-utilities($color-name, $var-name);
    /* Generates text-, bg-, and border- color utilities */
    Example:
        @include generate-color-utilities('primary', '--color-primary');
    Output:
        .text-primary { color: var(--color-primary); }
        .bg-primary { background-color: var(--color-primary); }
        .border-primary { border-color: var(--color-primary); }

----- RESPONSIVE MIXINS -----

@include generate-responsive-utilities($utilities-map, $breakpoint-name);
    /* Generates responsive variants of utilities */

----- STATE MIXINS -----

@include generate-state-utilities($utilities-map, $state);
    /* Generates state variants (hover, focus, etc.) */

@include generate-dark-mode-utilities($utilities-map);
    /* Generates dark mode variants */

@include generate-rtl-utilities($utilities-map);
    /* Generates RTL variants */

================================================================================
SECTION 14: TESTING & QUALITY ASSURANCE
================================================================================

ApexCSS includes a comprehensive testing infrastructure to ensure quality,
compatibility, and reliability across all framework features.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
UNIT TESTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Testing Framework: Mocha with sass-true
Location: tests/unit/

Available Commands:
    npm run test:unit          /* Run unit tests */
    npm run test:coverage      /* Run tests with coverage report */

Test Categories:
    - config-builder.test.js   /* Configuration generation tests */
    - utilities-mixins.test.js /* Sass mixin functionality tests */
    - generate-cli.test.js     /* CLI tool tests */

Unit tests validate:
    - Configuration parsing and validation
    - SCSS variable generation
    - Sass mixin output correctness
    - CLI argument handling

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
END-TO-END (E2E) TESTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Testing Framework: Playwright
Location: tests/e2e/

Setup:
    npm run test:e2e:install   /* Install Playwright and dependencies */

Available Commands:
    npm run test:e2e           /* Run e2e tests headlessly */
    npm run test:e2e:ui        /* Run tests with UI mode */

Test Coverage:
    - CSS custom properties (design tokens)
    - Theme switching (light/dark modes)
    - Responsive utilities across breakpoints
    - Layout utilities (display, flexbox, grid)
    - Visual utilities (colors, borders, shadows)
    - Interaction utilities (transitions, cursor)
    - Layer cascade ordering

Test Structure:
    tests/e2e/tests/
    ├── base/                  /* Base layer tests */
    ├── themes/                /* Theme and token tests */
    └── utilities/             /* Utility class tests */
        ├── core/              /* Essential utilities */
        └── extended/          /* Advanced utilities */

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CODE QUALITY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Stylelint Configuration:
    - SCSS linting: .stylelintrc.json
    - CSS linting: .stylelintrc-css.json

Available Commands:
    npm run lint:sass          /* Lint SCSS source files */
    npm run lint:css           /* Lint generated CSS output */

Linting Rules:
    - scss/dollar-variable-pattern: Enforce variable naming conventions
    - scss/at-rule-no-unknown: Validate SCSS syntax
    - declaration-property-value-no-unknown: Catch invalid CSS values
    - no-duplicate-selectors: Prevent selector duplication

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COVERAGE REPORTING
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Tool: c8 (V8 coverage)

Coverage Command:
    npm run test:coverage

Reports generated:
    - Text summary in console
    - LCOV report for CI integration
    - HTML report (coverage/)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONTINUOUS INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GitHub Actions workflows:
    - Automated testing on PRs
    - Style enforcement
    - Build verification
    - Cross-browser compatibility checks

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TEST-DRIVEN DEVELOPMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When contributing new utilities:

1. Write tests first (spec.ts or .test.js)
2. Implement the utility
3. Run tests to verify
4. Check coverage: npm run test:coverage
5. Lint: npm run lint:sass

Example: Adding a new utility
    /* tests/e2e/tests/utilities/core/layout/example.spec.ts */
    import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';
    
    test.describe('Example Utility', () => {
      test('applies correct styles', async ({ page }) => {
        await page.goto('/test-page');
        const element = page.locator('.test-element');
        await expect(element).toHaveCSS('property', 'value');
      });
    });

================================================================================
SECTION 15: BUILD SYSTEM & ENTRY POINTS
================================================================================

ENTRY FILES (src/):

| Entry File              | Output File       | Contents                           |
|-------------------------|-------------------|-----------------------------------|
| src/main.scss           | dist/apex.css     | Complete framework (all modules)  |
| src/entry-base.scss     | dist/base.css     | Base/reset styles only            |
| src/entry-utilities.scss| dist/utilities.css| Utility classes only              |
| src/entry-themes.scss   | dist/themes.css   | CSS custom properties only        |

MODULE FORWARDING (src/utilities/core/_index.scss):
    Core utilities are forwarded through _index.scss. When adding new utilities,
    ensure they are @forward'd in the appropriate _index.scss file:

    Example:
        @forward 'layout/display';    /* .block, .flex, .hidden, etc. */
        @forward 'layout/flexbox';     /* .flex-row, .justify-center, etc. */
        @forward 'layout/grid';        /* .grid-cols-*, .gap-*, etc. */
        @forward 'layout/positioning'; /* .absolute, .fixed, .inset-*, etc. */
        @forward 'layout/visibility';  /* .visible, .invisible, .collapse */

NPM SCRIPTS:
    npm run build              /* Build complete framework */
    npm run config:build       /* Generate SCSS from apex.config.js */
    npm run config:watch       /* Watch config and auto-rebuild */
    npm run dev                /* Start Vite dev server */
    npm run lint               /* Run Stylelint on SCSS */

WORKFLOW:
    1. Customize apex.config.js
    2. Run: npm run config:build
    3. Run: npm run build
    4. Use generated CSS in dist/

================================================================================
SECTION 16: FILE STRUCTURE
================================================================================

src/
├── base/                  /* Base/reset styles */
│   ├── _base.scss
│   └── _index.scss
├── config/                /* Configuration files */
│   ├── _custom-config.scss    /* Auto-generated (DO NOT EDIT) */
│   └── _index.scss
├── mixins/                /* SCSS mixins */
│   ├── _accessibility.scss
│   ├── _dark-mode.scss
│   ├── _index.scss
│   ├── _states.scss
│   └── _utilities.scss
├── plugins/               /* Animation keyframes */
│   ├── _animations.scss
│   └── _index.scss
├── themes/                /* Theme definitions */
│   ├── _custom.scss
│   ├── _dark.scss
│   ├── _default.scss
│   └── _index.scss
└── utilities/             /* All utility classes */
    ├── _functions.scss
    ├── _index.scss
    ├── core/              /* Essential utilities */
    │   ├── _index.scss    /* Forwards all core utilities */
    │   ├── accessibility/ /* Focus, contrast, motion, screen-reader */
    │   ├── interaction/   /* Cursor, transitions */
    │   ├── layout/        /* Display, flexbox, grid, positioning, visibility */
    │   ├── rtl/           /* Direction, spacing */
    │   ├── spacing/       /* Margin, padding, sizing */
    │   ├── typography/    /* Font utilities */
    │   └── visual/        /* Backgrounds, borders, colors, shadows, opacity */
    ├── extended/          /* Advanced utilities */
    │   ├── _index.scss
    │   ├── advanced/      /* Box, divide, logical-properties, svg, zoom */
    │   ├── content/       /* Break, counter, list, quotes, table */
    │   ├── effects/       /* Aspect-ratio, filters, transforms */
    │   ├── interaction/   /* Caret, scroll, snap, touch, will-change */
    │   ├── layout/        /* Columns, container-queries, float, isolation */
    │   ├── typography/    /* Font-features, hyphens, text-wrap, word-break */
    │   └── visual/        /* Accent-color, blend-modes, masks, ring, outline */
    └── states/            /* State variants (hover, focus, active, disabled) */

dist/                      /* Generated CSS output */
├── apex.css               /* Complete framework (~923KB) */
├── base.css               /* Base styles only (~2.5KB) */
├── utilities.css          /* Utilities only (~730KB) */
└── themes.css             /* Themes only (~190KB) */

tools/                     /* Build tools */
├── config-builder.js      /* Generates SCSS from apex.config.js */
└── generate.js            /* Utility generators */

docs/
├── CONFIG.md              /* Configuration guide */
└── LLMs.txt               /* This file */

================================================================================
SECTION 16.5: CONTRIBUTING GUIDELINES
================================================================================

ApexCSS welcomes contributions from the community. This section outlines the
standards and processes for contributing to the framework.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEVELOPMENT SETUP
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Fork and clone the repository:
    git clone https://github.com/chris-briddock/apex.git
    cd apex

2. Install dependencies:
    npm install

3. Build the framework:
    npm run config:build
    npm run build

4. Run tests to verify setup:
    npm run test:unit

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SASS/SCSS STANDARDS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Module System (REQUIRED):
    /* NEVER use @import - it's deprecated */
    @import "variables";  /* WRONG */

    /* ALWAYS use @use and @forward */
    @use "sass:math";
    @use "../tokens" as tokens;  /* CORRECT */

    /* Forward modules for public API */
    @forward "mixins";
    @forward "functions";

Naming Conventions:
    - Variables: $kebab-case (e.g., $primary-color)
    - Mixins: snake_case (e.g., @mixin generate-utilities)
    - Functions: snake_case (e.g., @function rem-calc)
    - Partials: _kebab-case.scss (e.g., _custom-config.scss)

File Organization:
    src/utilities/category/
    ├── _index.scss          /* Forwards all modules in category */
    └── _feature.scss        /* Individual utility implementations */

Nesting Rules:
    - Maximum 3 levels of nesting
    - Prefer flat, BEM-inspired naming over deep nesting
    - Use & for pseudo-classes and modifiers

Color Functions:
    /* NEVER use deprecated functions */
    darken($color, 10%);     /* WRONG - deprecated */
    lighten($color, 10%);    /* WRONG - deprecated */

    /* ALWAYS use color module */
    @use "sass:color";
    color.scale($color, $lightness: -10%);  /* CORRECT */

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CODE QUALITY REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before submitting a PR:

1. Lint your code:
    npm run lint:sass        /* Must pass with no errors */
    npm run lint:css         /* After building */

2. Run all tests:
    npm run test:unit
    npm run test:e2e

3. Check test coverage:
    npm run test:coverage    /* Maintain or improve coverage */

4. Build verification:
    npm run build            /* Must complete without errors */

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONTRIBUTING WORKFLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Create a feature branch:
    git checkout -b feature/my-new-feature

2. Make your changes following standards above

3. Add tests for new functionality:
    - Unit tests in tests/unit/
    - E2E tests in tests/e2e/tests/

4. Update documentation:
    - Update docs/LLMs.txt if adding new utilities
    - Update docs/CONFIG.md if adding configuration options

5. Commit with clear messages:
    feat: add new utility category
    fix: resolve spacing issue in grid utilities
    docs: update configuration examples
    test: add coverage for color utilities

6. Push and create a Pull Request

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PULL REQUEST REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your PR must:
    - Pass all CI checks (lint, test, build)
    - Include tests for new features
    - Update relevant documentation
    - Not break existing functionality
    - Follow the established code style

Review Process:
    - All PRs require review before merging
    - Address feedback promptly
    - Squash commits if requested

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REPORTING ISSUES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Bug Reports:
    - Use the GitHub issue tracker
    - Include reproduction steps
    - Specify browser/environment
    - Provide minimal test case

Feature Requests:
    - Describe the use case
    - Explain why existing utilities don't suffice
    - Consider implementation complexity

Security Issues:
    - Report privately to maintainers
    - Do not open public issues

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CODE OF CONDUCT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- Be respectful and inclusive
- Welcome newcomers and help them learn
- Focus on constructive feedback
- Credit others for their contributions
- Follow the golden rule

================================================================================
SECTION 17: COMMON PATTERNS
================================================================================

----- CARD COMPONENT -----
    <div class="bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md p-6 max-w-sm">
        <h2 class="text-xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-2">Card Title</h2>
        <p class="text-gray-600 mb-4">Card content goes here.</p>
        <button class="bg-primary-500 hover:bg-primary-600 text-white px-4 py-2 rounded-md transition-colors">
            Action
        </button>
    </div>

----- RESPONSIVE GRID -----
    <div class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4">
        <div class="bg-gray-100 p-4">Item 1</div>
        <div class="bg-gray-100 p-4">Item 2</div>
        <div class="bg-gray-100 p-4">Item 3</div>
    </div>

----- FLEXBOX CENTERING -----
    <div class="flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen">
        <div class="text-center">
            <h1 class="text-4xl font-bold mb-4">Centered Content</h1>
            <p class="text-gray-600">Perfectly centered both ways</p>
        </div>
    </div>

----- FORM STYLING -----
    <form class="space-y-4 max-w-md">
        <div>
            <label class="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1">Email</label>
            <input type="email" class="w-full px-4 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary-500">
        </div>
        <button type="submit" class="w-full bg-primary-500 hover:bg-primary-600 text-white font-medium py-2 px-4 rounded-md transition-colors">
            Submit
        </button>
    </form>

----- NAVIGATION BAR -----
    <nav class="bg-white shadow-sm">
        <div class="max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8">
            <div class="flex justify-between h-16 items-center">
                <div class="flex-shrink-0 font-bold text-xl">Logo</div>
                <div class="hidden md:flex space-x-8">
                    <a href="#" class="text-gray-700 hover:text-gray-900">Home</a>
                    <a href="#" class="text-gray-700 hover:text-gray-900">About</a>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    </nav>

----- DARK MODE TOGGLE -----
    <html class="dark">
        <body class="bg-white dark:bg-gray-900 text-gray-900 dark:text-white transition-colors">
            <!-- Content -->
        </body>
    </html>

    <script>
        function toggleDark() {
            document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark');
        }
    </script>

================================================================================
SECTION 18: DESIGN PRINCIPLES
================================================================================

1. MOBILE-FIRST: Default styles are for mobile; use responsive prefixes for larger screens

2. OKLCH COLOR SPACE: Uses perceptually uniform colors (not HSL/RGB)
   - lightness: 0-100% (uniform perceived brightness)
   - chroma: 0-0.4 (color intensity)
   - hue: 0-360 (color wheel angle)

3. CSS CUSTOM PROPERTIES: All theming uses CSS variables for runtime customization

4. MODULAR IMPORTS: Only include utilities you need via feature toggles

5. LOGICAL PROPERTIES: Full support for RTL with logical margin/padding

6. ACCESSIBILITY FIRST: Screen reader support, focus management, reduced motion

================================================================================
SECTION 17.5: ADVANCED CONFIGURATION
================================================================================

This section covers advanced configuration options and programmatic usage of
the ApexCSS config builder and generation tools.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONFIG BUILDER API
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

File: tools/config-builder.js

The config builder provides a programmatic API for generating SCSS configuration
from JavaScript configuration objects.

CLI Usage:
    npm run config:build       /* Build with default apex.config.js */
    npm run config:watch       /* Watch mode for development */

CLI Options:
    --config=<path>            /* Specify custom config file path */
    --init                     /* Create default config file */
    --watch                    /* Watch config file for changes */

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROGRAMMATIC API
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Import the config builder in Node.js scripts:

    import { ConfigBuilder } from './tools/config-builder.js';

    const builder = new ConfigBuilder({
      configPath: './apex.config.js',
      outputPath: './src/config/_custom-config.scss'
    });

    // Generate configuration
    await builder.build();

    // Watch for changes
    await builder.watch();

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CUSTOM UTILITY GENERATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

File: tools/generate.js

Generate custom utilities programmatically:

    import { generateUtilities } from './tools/generate.js';

    // Generate from a value map
    generateUtilities({
      property: 'display',
      values: {
        block: 'block',
        flex: 'flex',
        grid: 'grid',
        hidden: 'none'
      },
      classPrefix: '',
      outputPath: './src/custom/utilities.scss'
    });

    // Generate responsive variants
    generateUtilities({
      property: 'padding',
      values: { sm: '0.5rem', md: '1rem', lg: '2rem' },
      classPrefix: 'p',
      responsive: true,
      breakpoints: ['sm', 'md', 'lg']
    });

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CUSTOMIZING FEATURE TOGGLES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Advanced feature configuration with conditional logic:

    // apex.config.js
    export default {
      features: {
        // Core (always needed)
        display: true,
        flexbox: true,
        grid: true,
        spacing: true,
        typography: true,
        colors: true,
        
        // Extended (optional based on project)
        flexExtended: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
        gridExtended: true,
        animations: true,
        transforms: true,
        transforms3d: false,  /* Disable for smaller bundle */
        
        // Specialized (project-specific)
        containerQueries: true,
        logicalProperties: true,
        darkMode: true,
        rtl: true,
        
        // Accessibility (recommended)
        accessibility: true,
        reducedMotion: true,
        
        // Content utilities (optional)
        list: true,
        counter: false,
        quotes: false
      }
    };

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CUSTOM MIXINS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create custom Sass mixins for project-specific utilities:

    /* src/mixins/_custom.scss */
    @use "sass:map";
    @use "config" as config;

    @mixin custom-spacing-utilities {
      @each $name, $value in config.$custom-spacing {
        .space-#{$name} {
          margin: #{$value};
        }
      }
    }

    @mixin generate-responsive-custom($property, $values, $prefix: '') {
      @each $name, $value in $values {
        .#{$prefix}#{$name} {
          #{$property}: #{$value};
        }
        
        @each $bp, $width in config.$breakpoints {
          @media (width >= #{$width}) {
            .#{$bp}\:#{$prefix}#{$name} {
              #{$property}: #{$value};
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
BUILD HOOKS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Extend the build process with custom hooks:

    // apex.config.js
    export default {
      // ... standard config
      
      hooks: {
        beforeBuild: async () => {
          console.log('Running pre-build tasks...');
          // Custom preprocessing
        },
        
        afterBuild: async (stats) => {
          console.log(`Build complete: ${stats.fileCount} files`);
          // Post-processing, deployment, etc.
        }
      }
    };

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ENVIRONMENT-SPECIFIC CONFIGS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create different configurations for different environments:

    // apex.config.development.js
    export default {
      features: {
        // Include all for development
        ...allFeatures,
        arbitrary: true  /* Enable arbitrary values */
      },
      sourceMaps: true,
      minify: false
    };

    // apex.config.production.js
    export default {
      features: {
        // Minimal for production
        display: true,
        flexbox: true,
        grid: true,
        spacing: true,
        colors: true,
        // ... etc
      },
      minify: true,
      purgeUnused: true
    };

Build command:
    NODE_ENV=production npm run config:build -- --config=apex.config.production.js

================================================================================
SECTION 19: MODERN CSS FEATURES
================================================================================

ApexCSS supports cutting-edge CSS features that enhance user experience and
enable advanced layouts and interactions.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VIEW TRANSITIONS API
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The View Transitions API enables smooth, animated transitions between page
states without JavaScript animation libraries.

Browser Support: Chrome 126+, Edge 126+, Safari 18+ (behind flag)

Usage with ApexCSS:
    /* Enable view transitions */
    @view-transition {
      navigation: auto;
    }

    /* Custom transition styles */
    ::view-transition-old(root) {
      animation: fade-out 0.3s ease;
    }

    ::view-transition-new(root) {
      animation: fade-in 0.3s ease;
    }

ApexCSS utilities for View Transitions:
    view-transition-name-{name}    /* Set view-transition-name */
    view-transition-auto           /* Enable auto transitions */
    view-transition-none           /* Disable transitions */

Example:
    <div class="view-transition-name-card">
      <!-- Content transitions smoothly -->
    </div>

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCHOR POSITIONING
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CSS Anchor Positioning enables elements to be positioned relative to other
elements without JavaScript calculations.

Browser Support: Chrome 125+, Edge 125+, Firefox (in development)

ApexCSS Anchor Positioning Utilities:
    anchor-{name}                  /* Declare element as anchor */
    position-anchor-{name}         /* Position relative to anchor */
    position-area-{area}           /* Position within anchor area */

Position Areas:
    top, bottom, left, right
    center, center-top, center-bottom
    start, end, start-top, start-bottom
    end-top, end-bottom
    span-{direction}

Example:
    <!-- The anchor -->
    <button class="anchor-menu-trigger" id="menu-btn">
      Open Menu
    </button>

    <!-- Positioned element -->
    <div class="position-anchor-menu-trigger position-area-bottom">
      <!-- Menu content positioned below button -->
    </div>

Fallback Strategy:
    @supports not (anchor-name: --menu) {
      .position-area-bottom {
        position: absolute;
        top: 100%;
        left: 0;
      }
    }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
@SCOPE RULE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The @scope rule enables scoped CSS styling without affecting the rest of the
document - perfect for component isolation.

Browser Support: Chrome 118+, Edge 118+, Firefox 128+, Safari 17.2+

ApexCSS @scope Integration:
    /* Scoped component styles */
    @scope (.card) {
      :scope { /* The .card element itself */
        background: var(--color-surface);
        border-radius: var(--radius-lg);
      }
      
      /* Only matches .title inside .card */
      .title {
        font-size: var(--text-xl);
      }
      
      /* Scoped hover states */
      &:hover {
        box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg);
      }
    }

Utility for Scoped Containers:
    scope-isolated    /* Creates a scope boundary */
    scope-root        /* Defines scope root element */

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
POPOVER API
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Native popover functionality with CSS anchoring support.

Browser Support: Chrome 114+, Edge 114+, Firefox 125+, Safari 17+

ApexCSS Popover Utilities:
    popover-auto      /* Automatic popover behavior */
    popover-manual    /* Manual popover control */
    popover-target    /* Defines popover target */

Example:
    <button popovertarget="my-popover" class="btn-primary">
      Toggle Popover
    </button>
    <div id="my-popover" popover class="popover-auto p-4 rounded-lg shadow-xl">
      Popover content
    </div>

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SELECTLIST (EXPERIMENTAL)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Stylable select dropdowns using selectlist element.

Browser Support: Chrome 119+ (experimental), others pending

ApexCSS Selectlist Styles:
    selectlist        /* Base selectlist styling */
    selectlist-button /* Custom select button */
    selectlist-popup  /* Dropdown popup styling */
    selectlist-option /* Individual option styling */

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CSS NESTING
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Native CSS nesting without Sass preprocessing.

Browser Support: Chrome 112+, Edge 112+, Firefox 117+, Safari 16.5+

ApexCSS uses native nesting in dist output:
    /* Input (SCSS) */
    .button {
      background: blue;
      &:hover { background: darkblue; }
    }

    /* Output (CSS) */
    .button { background: blue; }
    .button:hover { background: darkblue; }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONTAINER QUERIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Component-level responsive design.

Browser Support: Chrome 105+, Edge 105+, Firefox 110+, Safari 16+

ApexCSS Container Query Utilities:
    @container-{name}    /* Define container context */
    @sm, @md, @lg, @xl   /* Container size breakpoints */

Example:
    <div class="@container-card">
      <div class="@md:grid-cols-2 @lg:grid-cols-3">
        <!-- Responsive based on container, not viewport -->
      </div>
    </div>

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASCADE LAYERS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Precise specificity management.

ApexCSS uses @layer for predictable cascade:
    @layer reset, tokens, base, utilities, themes;

Custom layer usage:
    @import 'apexcss' layer(utilities);
    
    @layer components {
      .my-component { /* Your styles */ }
    }

================================================================================
SECTION 20: COMPARISON WITH TAILWIND CSS
================================================================================

This section provides a comprehensive comparison between ApexCSS and Tailwind CSS
(v4.0 as of 2026-04-01), based on the latest documentation from Context7.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
19.1 PHILOSOPHY & APPROACH
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SIMILARITIES:
    - Utility-first approach: Build designs directly in HTML using utility classes
    - Responsive prefixes: Both use md:, lg:, xl: breakpoint prefixes
    - State variants: Both support hover:, focus:, active:, disabled:
    - Dark mode support: Both provide dark: variant for theme switching
    - Mobile-first responsive design: Default styles for mobile, larger screens override

KEY PHILOSOPHICAL DIFFERENCES:

    | Aspect              | ApexCSS                                    | Tailwind CSS v4                    |
    |---------------------|--------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
    | Core Philosophy     | CSS-first with Sass build                  | CSS-native with @theme directive    |
    | Design System       | OKLCH color space by default               | HSL/RGB, OKLCH via custom config     |
    | Configuration       | JavaScript config → SCSS generation        | CSS-first @theme block              |
    | Extensibility       | Sass mixins and @extend                    | @utility and @layer directives      |
    | Target Audience     | Sass/SCSS users, design systems            | All web developers, framework-agnostic|

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
19.2 CONFIGURATION COMPARISON
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

APEXCSS CONFIGURATION (apex.config.js):
    - JavaScript-based configuration file
    - Generates SCSS variables via npm run config:build
    - 104 feature toggles for granular control
    - Separate build step required for config changes

    Example:
    ```javascript
    export default {
      features: {
        display: true,
        flexbox: true,
        darkMode: true,
        // ... 100+ more toggles
      },
      colors: { /* custom colors */ },
      breakpoints: { /* custom breakpoints */ }
    }
    ```

TAILWIND CSS v4 CONFIGURATION (CSS-first):
    - CSS-native @theme directive (new in v4.0)
    - No separate config file required
    - Design tokens defined directly in CSS
    - Zero configuration to start

    Example:
    ```css
    @import "tailwindcss";

    @theme {
      --color-mint-500: oklch(0.72 0.11 178);
      --font-script: Great Vibes, cursive;
      --breakpoint-3xl: 120rem;
      --animate-fade-in: fade-in 0.3s ease-out;

      @keyframes fade-in {
        0% { opacity: 0; }
        100% { opacity: 1; }
      }
    }
    ```

CONFIGURATION COMPARISON TABLE:

    | Feature                    | ApexCSS                              | Tailwind CSS v4                    |
    |----------------------------|--------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
    | Config Location            | apex.config.js                       | CSS file with @theme                |
    | Config Format              | JavaScript                           | CSS-native                          |
    | Build Step Required        | Yes (config:build + build)           | No (zero config)                    |
    | Feature Toggles             | 104 granular toggles                 | corePlugins in JS or CSS @layer     |
    | Custom Colors              | JS config → SCSS variables            | @theme --color-* variables          |
    | Custom Breakpoints         | JS config → SCSS variables            | @theme --breakpoint-* variables     |
    | Custom Fonts               | JS config → SCSS variables            | @theme --font-* variables           |
    | Custom Animations          | SCSS files                            | @theme with @keyframes              |
    | Theme Variables            | CSS custom properties generated       | Native CSS custom properties        |
    | Multiple Themes            | CSS variable switching                | CSS variable switching              |

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
19.3 BUILD SYSTEM & PERFORMANCE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

APEXCSS:
    - Build Tool: Sass/SCSS compilation (Dart Sass)
    - Bundler: Vite v8
    - Output: Static CSS file (dist/apex.css)
    - PurgeCSS: Optional, configured via purgecss.config.js
    - JIT: No JIT compilation (pre-generated classes)
    - Bundle Size: ~50KB gzipped (full), smaller with feature toggles

TAILWIND CSS v4:
    - Build Tool: PostCSS + Lightning CSS (new in v4)
    - Bundler: Vite, Webpack, or standalone CLI
    - Output: JIT-compiled CSS (only used classes)
    - PurgeCSS: Built-in content detection
    - JIT: JIT by default (compiles only what you use)
    - Bundle Size: ~10-30KB (depends on usage)

BUILD COMPARISON TABLE:

    | Feature                    | ApexCSS                              | Tailwind CSS v4                    |
    |----------------------------|--------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
    | Build Tool                 | Dart Sass                            | PostCSS + Lightning CSS             |
    | JIT Compilation            | No (pre-generated)                   | Yes (by default)                    |
    | Content Detection          | Manual PurgeCSS config               | Automatic @source detection         |
    | Zero Config Setup          | Requires config file                 | Yes (@import "tailwindcss")         |
    | Build Speed                | Fast (Sass compilation)              | Very fast (Lightning CSS)           |
    | Watch Mode                 | Via Vite/Webpack                     | Built-in CLI watch mode             |
    | Minification               | Via bundler                          | Built-in (--minify)                 |
    | Source Maps                | Via bundler                          | Built-in support                    |
    | Browser Prefixing          | Via PostCSS/Autoprefixer             | Built-in (Lightning CSS)            |

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
19.4 UTILITY CLASS COMPARISON
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CORE UTILITIES (Both frameworks):

    | Category          | ApexCSS Classes         | Tailwind CSS Classes      | Notes                    |
    |-------------------|-------------------------|---------------------------|--------------------------|
    | Display           | block, flex, etc.       | block, flex, etc.         | Identical naming         |
    | Flexbox           | flex-row, items-center  | flex-row, items-center    | Nearly identical         |
    | Grid              | grid-cols-*, gap-*      | grid-cols-*, gap-*        | Nearly identical         |
    | Spacing           | p-*, m-*, px-*, mx-*    | p-*, m-*, px-*, mx-*      | Identical scale          |
    | Sizing            | w-*, h-*, min-w-*, max-w-*| w-*, h-*, min-w-*, max-w-*| Identical              |
    | Typography        | text-*, font-*          | text-*, font-*            | Similar naming           |
    | Colors            | text-*, bg-*            | text-*, bg-*              | Different color systems  |
    | Borders           | border-*, rounded-*     | border-*, rounded-*       | Nearly identical         |
    | Shadows           | shadow-*                | shadow-*                  | Similar scale            |
    | Opacity           | opacity-*               | opacity-*                 | Identical                |
    | Transitions       | transition-*            | transition-*              | Similar                  |

APEXCSS-SPECIFIC UTILITIES (Not in Tailwind core):

    | Category                  | Classes                              | Purpose                              |
    |---------------------------|--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
    | Logical Properties        | ms-*, me-*, ps-*, pe-*              | RTL-aware margin/padding             |
    | Sizing Logical            | inline-size-*, block-size-*         | Logical width/height                 |
    | Border Radius Logical     | rounded-s-*, rounded-e-*             | RTL-aware border radius              |
    | CSS Columns               | columns-*, column-count-*            | Multi-column layouts                  |
    | Writing Mode              | writing-mode-*                       | Vertical text, CJK support           |
    | Print Utilities           | page-break-*, orphans-*, widows-*    | Print-specific styles                |
    | Counter Utilities         | counter-reset-*, counter-increment-* | CSS counters                          |
    | Quote Utilities           | quotes-*                             | Quotation mark styling               |
    | Touch Action              | touch-*                              | Touch gesture control                |
    | Caret Color               | caret-*                              | Text cursor color                    |
    | Overscroll Behavior       | overscroll-*                         | Scroll chaining control              |
    | Hyphens                   | hyphens-*                            | Word hyphenation                     |
    | Text Orientation          | text-orientation-*                   | Vertical text orientation            |
    | Unicode Bidi              | unicode-bidi-*                       | Bidirectional text                   |
    | Hanging Punctuation       | hanging-punctuation-*                | Punctuation hanging                  |
    | Initial Letter            | initial-letter-*                     | Drop caps, initial letters           |
    | Font Variant Numeric      | font-variant-numeric-*               | Numeric typography                   |
    | Font Features             | font-feature-settings-*             | OpenType features                    |
    | Font Smoothing            | font-smoothing-*                    | Antialiasing control                 |
    | Mask Utilities            | mask-*, mask-image-*                 | CSS masks                            |
    | Color Scheme              | color-scheme-*                       | Browser color scheme                 |
    | Field Sizing              | field-sizing-*                       | New CSS field-sizing property        |
    | Shape Outside             | shape-outside-*                     | Text wrapping shapes                 |
    | Zoom                       | zoom-*                               | Zoom transform                       |
    | Marker Styling            | marker-*                             | List marker styling                  |
    | Box Decoration Break      | box-decoration-break-*              | Fragmentation control                |
    | Transition Behavior       | transition-behavior-*                | transition-behavior property         |
    | Image Rendering           | image-rendering-*                   | Image scaling algorithm              |

TAILWIND CSS v4-SPECIFIC FEATURES (Not in ApexCSS):

    | Feature                   | Description                                                    |
    |---------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
    | @theme Directive          | CSS-native configuration without JS config file                |
    | @utility Directive         | Define custom utilities that work with variants               |
    | @source Directive          | Explicit content path configuration                            |
    | @source not Directive      | Exclude paths from content scanning                            |
    | Lightning CSS              | Built-in vendor prefixing and modern syntax transforms         |
    | Arbitrary Properties       | Full support for [any-css-property:any-value]                 |
    | Arbitrary Variants         | [&:nth-child(3)]:bg-red-500 for any selector                  |
    | Container Queries          | @container and container:* variants (Apex has this too)        |
    | Gradient Color Stops       | Extensive gradient utilities with arbitrary stops              |
    | Object-Position            | Object position utilities                                      |

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
19.5 RESPONSIVE DESIGN COMPARISON
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

BREAKPOINTS:

    | Framework    | Default Breakpoints                                        |
    |--------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
    | ApexCSS      | xs: 0px, sm:320px, md:768px, lg:1024px, xl:1280px, xxl:2560px, xxxl:3840px, ultra:7680px |
    | Tailwind v4  | sm:640px, md:768px, lg:1024px, xl:1280px, 2xl:1536px       |

RESPONSIVE SYNTAX:

    ApexCSS:
        md:flex        /* display: flex at md breakpoint and up */
        lg:p-8         /* padding: 2rem at lg breakpoint and up */
        sm:hidden      /* display: none at sm breakpoint and up */

    Tailwind CSS v4:
        md:flex        /* display: flex at md breakpoint and up */
        lg:p-8         /* padding: 2rem at lg breakpoint and up */
        sm:hidden      /* display: none at sm breakpoint and up */

CONTAINER QUERIES (Both frameworks):

    ApexCSS:
        @container     /* Define a container query context */
        @sm:grid-cols-2 /* Apply at container breakpoint */

    Tailwind CSS v4:
        @container     /* Define a container query context */
        @md:grid-cols-2 /* Apply at container breakpoint */

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
19.6 DARK MODE COMPARISON
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

APEXCSS DARK MODE:
    - Supports both class and media query strategies simultaneously
    - CSS custom properties for theme switching
    - Built-in dark theme color palette
    - Automatic color scheme detection

    Usage:
    ```html
    <html class="dark">
      <body class="bg-white dark:bg-gray-900 text-gray-900 dark:text-white">
        <!-- Content -->
      </body>
    </html>
    ```

TAILWIND CSS v4 DARK MODE:
    - Supports class OR media query strategy (configurable)
    - dark: variant for any utility
    - Works with all variants (hover, focus, responsive)
    - prefers-color-scheme: dark media query support

    Usage:
